get.summary: List OPEN and ONHOLD Tickets Count
get.description: This API lists the number of open and onhold tickets assigned to specified agentIds in the given department. Do not give light agents in the list of agentIds since tickets are not assigned to light agents

parameters.description: Comma-separated array of agent IDs. Maximum number of IDs allowed is 50.

schema.description: Comma-separated array of agent IDs. Maximum number of IDs allowed is 50.

x-custom-parameters-attributes: Comma-separated array of agent IDs. Maximum number of IDs allowed is 50.

parameters.description: ID of the department from which the ticket count must be fetched

schema.description: ID of the department from which the ticket count must be fetched

422.description: This errorCode value appears if the input does not fulfil the conditions necessary for successfully executing the API.

This errorCode value appears if there are inaccuracies, such as duplicate entries or invalid IDs, in the data given by the user. The type of inaccuracy is identified by the errorType param.
The possible values for the errorType key are:
*invalid* - the field contains an invalid value *duplicate* - the field contains a value that already exists *missing* - the field does not contain a value
The field that contains the inaccurate value is returned in the fieldName param, which is represented in the JSON Pointer convention

value.message: The data does not comply to the validation restrictions defined.

get.description: This API lists a particular number of tickets that are associated to you from your help desk, based on the limit specified.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Key that returns additional information related to a ticket. Values allowed are: contacts, products, and assignee. All three values can be passed by separating them with a comma in the API request.

schema.description: Key that returns additional information related to a ticket. Values allowed are: contacts, products, and assignee. All three values can be passed by separating them with a comma in the API request.

parameters.name: follower
parameters.description: Filter by followers of the ticket. Values allowed : a valid @agentId@.

schema.description: Filter by followers of the ticket. Values allowed : a valid @agentId@.

parameters.name: departmentId
parameters.description: ID of the department from which the ticket count must be fetched

schema.description: ID of the department from which the ticket count must be fetched

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of tickets to fetch

schema.description: Number of tickets to fetch

parameters.name: sharedDepartmentId
parameters.description: Fetches only the tickets shared from @departmentId@ to @sharedDepartmentId@. If @departmentId@ is not provided, fetches all the tickets shared to @sharedDepartmentId@. @sharedDepartmentId@ is given precedence only when @isShared@ is @false@/not given.

schema.description: Fetches only the tickets shared from @departmentId@ to @sharedDepartmentId@. If @departmentId@ is not provided, fetches all the tickets shared to @sharedDepartmentId@. @sharedDepartmentId@ is given precedence only when @isShared@ is @false@/not given.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Index number, starting from which the tickets must be listed

schema.description: Index number, starting from which the tickets must be listed

parameters.name: assignee
parameters.description: assignee - Key that filters tickets by assignee. Values allowed are @Unassigned@ or a valid @assigneeId@. Multiple @assigneeIds@ can be passed as comma-separated values.

schema.description: assignee - Key that filters tickets by assignee. Values allowed are @Unassigned@ or a valid @assigneeId@. Multiple @assigneeIds@ can be passed as comma-separated values.

parameters.name: receivedInDays
parameters.description: Fetches recent tickets, based on @customer response time@. Values allowed are @15@, @30@ , @90@.

schema.description: Fetches recent tickets, based on @customer response time@. Values allowed are @15@, @30@ , @90@.

parameters.name: isShared
parameters.description: Filter all shared tickets actually belonging to @departmentId@ if @isShared@ is true. If @departmentId@ is not provided and if @isShared@ is true, shared tickets from all permitted departments will be listed. If @departmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @true@, tickets belonging to @departmentId@, but shared to any other department will be fetched. If @departmentId@ and @sharedDepartmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @true@, then tickets belonging to @departmentId@, but shared to @sharedDepartmentId@ will be fetched(Here permission checks are done on @departmentId@). If @departmentId@ and @sharedDepartmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @false@/not given, then tickets shared to @sharedDepartmentId@, but belonging to @departmentId@ will be fetched(Here permission checks are done on @sharedDepartmentId@).

schema.description: Filter all shared tickets actually belonging to @departmentId@ if @isShared@ is true. If @departmentId@ is not provided and if @isShared@ is true, shared tickets from all permitted departments will be listed. If @departmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @true@, tickets belonging to @departmentId@, but shared to any other department will be fetched. If @departmentId@ and @sharedDepartmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @true@, then tickets belonging to @departmentId@, but shared to @sharedDepartmentId@ will be fetched(Here permission checks are done on @departmentId@). If @departmentId@ and @sharedDepartmentId@ is provided and @isShared@ is @false@/not given, then tickets shared to @sharedDepartmentId@, but belonging to @departmentId@ will be fetched(Here permission checks are done on @sharedDepartmentId@).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by resolution status of the ticket. You can include multiple values by separating them with a comma

schema.description: Filter by resolution status of the ticket. You can include multiple values by separating them with a comma

parameters.name: commenter
parameters.description: Filter by commenters of the ticket. Values allowed : a valid @agentId@. As of now, either @follower@ or @commenter@ is supported. If both params are given, @commenter@ will be ignored.

schema.description: Filter by commenters of the ticket. Values allowed : a valid @agentId@. As of now, either @follower@ or @commenter@ is supported. If both params are given, @commenter@ will be ignored.

get.summary: Get colliding users action and chat status in a ticket
get.description: This API should be called after ticket is rendered in detailed view. This returns collision users (agent Id, their action in ticket and author details) and chat status of that collision. 'chatId' returns the uniqueId of the chat that belongs to a collision session. This chatId should be used only for publishing messages within the chat members. 'chatUsers' returns the agentIds of current users in chat. The colliding users action is identified by 2 arrays, 'collidingUsers' returns the agentIds of users colliding in ticket and 'usersAction' returns the action of corresponding colliding users in the same order respectively. The values 'collidingUsers' and 'usersAction' will be deprecated soon (DONOT USE THESE VALUES)
Get online agents ticket count

422.description:
This errorCode value appears if there are inaccuracies, such as duplicate entries or invalid IDs, in the data given by the user. The type of inaccuracy is identified by the errorType param.
The possible values for the errorType key are:
*invalid* - the field contains an invalid value *duplicate* - the field contains a value that already exists *missing* - the field does not contain a value
The field that contains the inaccurate value is returned in the fieldName param, which is represented in the JSON Pointer convention

get.summary: Get Shared Access Details based on Sharing type
get.description:
This API fetches the different access rights configured for the sharing modes available in Zoho Desk.
The sharing modes are: READ_WRITE, READ_ONLY, and RESTRICTED_ACCESS. 
Possible Keys
forward - Boolean - Determines if a private reply, private thread, or a draft can be forwarded;
sendReply - Boolean - Determines if a public reply and comment can be made;
printThread - Boolean - Determines if a thread can be printed
showOriginal - Boolean - Determines if the More Actions set that belongs to a thread can be accessed
allowedIntegratedActions - List - Values allowed: NONE, ALL
unWritableProperties - List - Values allowed: NONE, ALL, or set of properties to be restricted
readableSubEntities - List - Values allowed: NONE, ALL, or set of sub-entities that can be accessed
unReadableProperties - List - Values allowed: NONE, ALL, or set of properties to be restricted
unWritableSubEntities - List - Values allowed: NONE, ALL, or set of sub-entities to be restricted

This API does not fetch access rights configured for viewing Auto Suggestions, Timeline, and Agent Collision.
This API lists tickets received from a specific products.

badRequestErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the authentication token in the API request is invalid

value.message: You are not authorized to perform this operation.

unauthorizedErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the authentication token in the API request is invalid

value.message: You are not authorized to perform this operation.

invalidOauthErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the OAuthToken is invalid or expired.

value.message: The OauthToken is invalid or has expired.

scopeMisMatchErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the OAuthToken does not contain the scope required to perform the operation.

value.message: The OAuthToken does not contain the scope required to perform this operation.

oauthOrgMisMatchErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the user chooses a different organization while generating the OAuthToken instead of the organization in which they want to perform the operation.

value.message: The OAuthToken is not valid for specified organization.

forbiddenErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the user does not have all the permissions required to access the resource.

value.message: You are not authorized to access this resource.

urlNotFoundErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the URL provided is invalid or does not exist.

value.message: The URL you requested could not be found.

methodNotAllowedErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the method in the API request is known by the server but disabled for the API requested.

value.message: This HTTP method is not allowed on this URL.

resourceSizeExceededErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the size of the resource exceeds the limit defined by the server.

value.message: Attachment size is too large. The maximum size supported is 20 MB.

unSupportedMediaTypeErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the server refuses the request as a result of receiving the input in an unsupported format.

value.message: The given content type is not supported. Please provide the input Content-Type as application/json

unprocessableEntityErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the input does not fulfil the conditions necessary for successfully executing the API.

value.message: The value passed for the 'from' query parameter is invalid.

missingEntityErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the input does not contain the mandatory parameters for successfully executing the API.

value.message: The mandatory parameter 'permission' is missing

thresholdExceedErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the user sends too many requests within a given amount of time ("rate limiting").

value.message: The maximum number of API calls that can be made per day has been exceeded.

concurrentRateLimitExceededErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the user fires too many requests in a simultaneous/concurrent manner ("rate limiting").

value.message: The maximum number of Concurrent API Calls that can be made has been exceeded.

internalErrorResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the server encounters an unexpected condition that prevents it from fulfilling the request.

value.message: An internal server error occurred while performing this operation.

licenseErrorResponse.description:
This errorCode value appears if the user is unable to perform an action due to the limitations of the Zoho Desk edition in use
The possible values for the feature key are
    *AGENTS*
    *LIGHT_AGENTS*
    *TEAM*
The possible values for the editionType key are
    *FREE*
    *PROFESSIONAL*
    *ENTERPRISE*

value.message: Your Zoho Desk Edition does not support this feature/operation.

multipleUnprocessEntityResponse.description: This errorCode value appears if the input does not fulfil the conditions necessary for successfully executing the API.

invalidDataErrorResponse.description:
This errorCode value appears if there are inaccuracies, such as duplicate entries or invalid IDs, in the data given by the user. The type of inaccuracy is identified by the errorType param.
The possible values for the errorType key are:
*invalid* - the field contains an invalid value *duplicate* - the field contains a value that already exists *missing* - the field does not contain a value
The field that contains the inaccurate value is returned in the fieldName param, which is represented in the JSON Pointer convention

value.message: The data does not comply to the validation restrictions defined.
